summarization.a: The figure shows ToF-ERDA depth profiles of the pristine MnO₂ film, where Mn and O are uniformly distributed and no lithium signal is detected, confirming the unlithiated starting structure.
summarization.b: After 10 lithiation cycles (10cLi–MnO₂), a weak lithium signal appears near the surface, indicating initial lithium incorporation and the onset of a surface-modified layer.
summarization.c: At 50 lithiation cycles (50cLi–MnO₂), the lithium concentration increases and penetrates further into the film, forming a clear core–shell structure with lithium-rich surface regions.
summarization.d: By 100 lithiation cycles (100cLi–MnO₂), lithium is still largely confined to the near-surface region, while the film interior remains relatively lithium-poor.
summarization.e: At 200 lithiation cycles (200cLi–MnO₂), lithium penetrates through most of the film thickness, approaching the Si substrate, indicating bulk lithiation.
summarization.f: After 300 lithiation cycles (300cLi–MnO₂), lithium is uniformly distributed throughout the entire film thickness, reaching the Si substrate and confirming complete conversion to lithium manganese oxide.

d.question: Contrast the material structure between a partially lithiated film (e.g., 100cLi) and a fully lithiated film (300cLi). What key structural benefit does full lithiation provide?
d.answer: Partial lithiation creates a layered structure with only the surface enriched in Li. Full lithiation produces a uniform bulk composition. This homogeneity improves mechanical and electrochemical stability.

d.question: The image emphasizes the high purity of the gas-phase lithiated films. What evidence from the data supports this claim, and why is such purity critical for a battery cathode application?
d.answer: Depth profiles show very low impurity levels. High purity minimizes side reactions, improves cycling stability, and is essential for achieving high performance and long life in battery cathodes.

c.question: Using the image and the depth profiles, report how the lithium content in the near-surface region increases across the 10cLi, 50cLi, and 100cLi samples. Also, identify the final crystalline phase formed after 300 cycles.
c.answer: Lithium in the top layer rises from 0.6 at.% (10c) to 3.2 at.% (50c) to 9.6 at.% (100c). After 300 cycles, the film becomes stoichiometric LiMn₂O₄.

a.question: Based on the elemental depth profiles in the given chart, describe how the spatial distribution of lithium evolves within the MnO₂ film as the number of LiOᵗBu–H₂O ALD cycles increases from 10 to 300.
a.answer: Lithium is initially confined to the surface region after 10–100 cycles. It gradually penetrates deeper, achieving full, homogeneous distribution throughout the entire film after 200–300 cycles.
